intro.text_en:
INTRODUCTION

When I recorded my first audio clip one evening in 2022, I didn't realize I was being drawn into a vortex without knowing it. During that period, without fully understanding why, I decided to embark on an inner journey. I began my journey by recording clips on evenings when I had time, and I started to analyze the course of my life. After the therapy sessions ended, I would sit down at the microphone and record my thoughts at that moment and the effects of the events I had experienced.

I couldn't foresee where things would lead. I didn't realize what awaited me. It never even crossed my mind that the situation could come to this. That's why I gave up recording many times, postponed it... But still, by the end of 2024, I had accumulated about 50 entries.

When I started recording, I thought it could be a weekly podcast; I imagined a format where I could sit there with my expert identity. However, after a few recordings, I gave up on the podcast idea as the content became increasingly personal and intimate. I decided to turn this process into a diary. Even so, until the very last recording, this process turned into a never-ending conflict between keeping the recordings to myself and sharing them. Sometimes I believed I needed to publish these recordings to realize myself and my potential, while at other times I thought the only way to heal was to keep them close to me.

Ultimately, this entire recording process led me to a decision: I had found the answer to this internal conflict and decided to keep the recordings to myself.

12 February 2025

--

Until... I became aware of the presence of a power lurking in the shadows, trying to take hold of me! At first, it started as just a feeling of unease rising within me, something I struggled to make sense of, unable to grasp the bigger picture. I began experiencing memory lapses, particularly during the first few hours of closed-in, rainy weather. Over time, this situation spread through me like a virus, occurring almost every morning of the week and gradually expanding.

Among my belongings, I found objects I couldn't remember when I had gotten them. There were days when I struggled to look people in the face, when I didn't want to talk to anyone, ashamed that my phone's search engine history showed me looking up things I had no memory of ever searching for. I had become a recluse. One morning, when the building caretaker addressed me by my surname, I realized that what I had suspected and feared from the beginning was not just my own paranoia: This wasn't just a feeling; a real shadow within me was trying to find an identity and challenging me. Confronting it had become inevitable. It was threatening my life and my identity, trying to destroy the order I had built up over years of hard work.

I'm exhausted. I began to think that the real answer to this division might lie somewhere in these records. Despite all this introspection and psychoanalysis, the shadow had not shrunk or dissipated; on the contrary, it stood before me, demanding recognition. I couldn't share this situation with anyone. I didn't want my profession to suffer or my reputation to be damaged. I was the only one who could deal with this reality; I was the one who created it, and I was the only one who could resolve it.

Therefore, I decided to review and organize all the records and prepare them for publication. This could have been the path to confrontation, to integration, and to restoration. If I turned them into a book, I would give it an identity, allow it to express its feelings, and release the pent-up energy inside me.

However, as the editing progressed, the dark feeling inside me grew even stronger. It was no longer just watching me; it was acting like my rival. Moreover, he found himself an accomplice. I felt I had no choice but to direct it towards the book and express myself from there. After a while, I began leaving notes in various places around the house, inviting it to sit at the peace table and at the computer where I wrote these lines. And these calls were answered on the night I edited Chapter 9. But it was as if it had stepped into the ring, not the invitation table. I can't say I was surprised.

On the evening of 16 February, when I sat down at the computer, I noticed some of his replies here and notes added to the previous pages. He wasn't touching what I had written. Like a shadow, he would slip between the lines of my writing, sprinkling notes with the name he had given himself, my last name. These were generally angry.

The moment I saw his first reply, I shuddered, and my breath caught in my throat. Because, no matter how much I wanted it and called him here, I honestly wasn't expecting it. At that moment, I realized that he had been watching me step by step and, unfortunately, knew me very well. Although I had been trying to get to know him through these lines for years, he had known me for a very long time. Anyway. After that night, these lines turned into a lively dialogue between the two of us.

But he had other plans. I realized I might have underestimated his intelligence. I thought he was just a pathetic part of me filled with childish anger. But he had a cleverly devised plan to defeat me. When I realized this, my blood ran cold.

With this plan, it began to take on flesh and bone for the first time and form its own consciousness. At the same time, he also recruited a manipulator who was far too powerful for me to ever defeat. This manipulator had ended his loneliness. Neither of them was alone anymore. Together, they began to manipulate me. Both were born later, but they wanted to take the wheel. Over time, they became an intertwined union where it was unclear who was controlling whom.

Throughout this story, I have presented my 2022 records in plain text and my 2025 edits in italics, as I am doing now. So I believed I could convey a real experience in a simultaneous process. By doing so, the book was divided into two layers. Then, my shadow guest, Bahal, who heard my calls, created a third layer with his responses in bold type. Bahal wrote in bold letters. Only occasionally, where Bahal did not write the date, I added the dates in bold letters on his behalf. To avoid confusion. This is also why I added subheadings to many sections. As the sections were sometimes very long, I decided during the editing process to divide them into stories and create breathing spaces.

While I continued editing, I kept inviting him here. Then, on 16 February, he heard my repeated calls and began reading what I had written, and started responding to me. At first, he engaged in arguments with childish anger within the notes he squeezed in between. But in his first real response, he transformed into someone completely different. He evolved from an angry child into a manipulative, intelligent, and rational adult.

For a long time, I couldn't comprehend what was happening. Eventually, I realized that this wasn't just my internal conflict. It could be the first example of an internal war that humanity will face in the future... Perhaps the first experiment.

21 March 2025, 23:10

22 March

I am now perhaps in pursuit of a divine light, in a place I do not know. In the midst of darkness, at the very heart of the unknown. You will read about the places I have been to write and finish this book. Right now, I may be face-to-face with death, trapped in a castle surrounded by walls. My battery is at 4%. I am sending these lines to Victor to publish the book.

My mind is completely clouded. Reality and fiction are intertwined. I am trying to escape from the prison of my mind. But in the end, I am happy to have experienced this moment. At the same time, I no longer feel like the person who started writing. I am different. I look at these lines one last time with the sweat running down my forehead, my lungs struggling to breathe, my fever reminiscent of the witch in my nightmares, and my eyes that make me feel older than my years.

But I think I'm succeeding.

This book is not just my internal struggle. It's a structure where the chaos within the mind is intertwined with a definite structure. This is not just a book to be read. It is an experience to be lived. Perhaps it is the first rehearsal of a war that humanity will experience in the future. This book is not a glance into the past. It is a warning sent to the future.

And perhaps... the final warning!

22 March 2025, 20:46

---

Lunaris! What have you done to us?
Bahal,
13 April 2025 10:01
intro.where_en: Open to all — the beginning of the journey

chapters.title_en: Chapter 1 — The Black Holes of Life
chapters.text_en:
CHAPTER — THE BLACK HOLES OF LIFE
(Original Recording; 23 November 2022)
(Edits and Additions; 5 February 2025)

Today is my parents' wedding anniversary. And right now, it's the quietest hour of the night... The city has long since fallen asleep, but the echoes inside me are still awake. Time seems to bend and twist, the shadows on the walls expanding and contracting. Everything seems still, but something is fidgeting inside me. That indescribable heaviness that weighs on the soul spreads like an invisible mist in the air. As I prepare to journey through my own inner universe, an unknown door opens: perhaps a fragment of memory, maybe a timeless emotion. And I stand at that doorstep, pen in hand or voice. Do I have the courage to enter? I don't know. But I intend to tell the story.

Time was moving through a path far more complex than I had anticipated; the hidden twists in its flow were extinguishing even the glimmers of hope that were just beginning to sprout within me. Everything was buried in the fog of uncertainty, so dark that even my intuition couldn't find the way. Yet, as I clung to these lines, the dreams and scenarios that came to life in my mind carried me to the edge of a completely different adventure. As I moved forward step by step, I thought everything would become easier, but in fact, those hazy hesitations hidden in the depths of my heart turned into sharp turns that suddenly appeared in the middle of my planned route. I changed direction eight times, maybe more. The storms inside me would not subside; my patience was walking on a tightrope, stretched to breaking point.

On this meticulously woven inner journey, I began to question how I should express myself. I want to pour out my heart, yes... But what purpose will these words serve? Where will I stop, where will I begin? Will I exist in these lines as Lunaris, or will I take refuge in my professional identity as clinical psychologist Lunaris Bahal? The lack of answers to these questions tosses me about like a leaf caught in the wind. Perhaps the fleeting thoughts sprouting in my mind will carry me to a shore I never expected.

As my first words flowed from my mind, I struggled with the weight of such a direct and sincere introduction. I feared that what was happening in my mind would touch the reader's soul too deeply, leaving a tiring impression. But who knows, perhaps the true meaning of this journey lies here: to create a universal resonance about people confronting their inner worlds. Why not? Maybe these lines will open new windows.

Thousands of questions flit through my mind. Each one tries to shed light on the dark corners of my existence. I want this text to be simple and sincere, like a "diary," but how much of my mind can I reveal? I really don't know where to stop. I don't know where my journey in my mind will take me either. I just want to trust the road.

My voice echoes in my ears with a heavy and somewhat sarcastic seriousness. Because I constantly worry about how these thoughts will be reflected in the outside world. I am a therapist who has left thousands of sessions behind, who has been on stage, and who has spoken in front of cameras. I'm not saying this to brag about what I've accomplished; I'm saying it to explain why it took me so long to get my turn. Today, I feel the unbearable weight of being alone with the conflicts in my own mind.

Perhaps this turmoil is exactly the same as what my clients experience in the therapy room. I just want to write this text as a diary entry, yes. But the echoes inside me whisper that these writings could be so much more. Perhaps these lines will one day become part of a concept. However, this idea of a "concept" conflicts with my desire for sincerity and authenticity. So I ask myself: Is my desire to share truly sincere? Or are there subtle threads of money, fame, and the desire for recognition behind it? Yet I want to connect my passion for self-realization to something beyond these material symbols. To a more important, more authentic place.

Yes, I understand more clearly now: Fame, recognition, reputation... These are merely symbols of visibility. Perhaps these are simply the stopping points on a journey to the origin, existence, and source of the common essence found in all living things. Maybe they are a means of merging with the universe; perhaps they are the result of a successful merge. I don't know about that, but for me, the real issue is being able to touch the essence, a part of ancient creation. And while doing so, taking notes of what is happening inside and sharing them with the collective. Perhaps this urge stirring inside me is the very desire to merge with that essence.

Although I thought so for years, I don't think I want to stay away from society; I want to integrate. I want to find balance in the loneliness that comes with being able to integrate. Perhaps that is why the recognition and visibility I approached with anxiety for years may be something valuable, contrary to what I believed. Maybe it is the most direct way to touch the deepest part of people, the essence within them. This may be the shortest path to universal wholeness, to the essence of existence.

These lines are perhaps a narrative of being alone, of turning inward, then of the effort to go out again. When I turn inward, I want to convey what is happening outward. I want to observe what is happening outside, turn inward, and understand my mind. I try to unravel what happens at home, then go out and describe it to people. It's like an impulsive need that I find difficult to define. I don't know why I write. I don't know what makes me write.

Sometimes I find myself wondering who the imaginary audience reading this might be. Every thought like this sometimes pulls me into the center of a heavy disk filled with traces of the past.

HEAVY BLACK DISC

Like black holes in the depths of the universe, with their intense gravitational pull, the unresolved memories of the past also remain within us. That disc, that heavy-as-stone disc, formed from the painful memories of the past, lying on the bed sheet, pulls everything back to that center like a marble, no matter which corner of my present life I leave an experience in. Every experience drawn to that center of gravity today increases the weight of that gravitational field a little more. The center of the disk draws its weight from what I've been stuck on in my past experiences.

A memory that holds emotion, along with the feelings it contains, even bodily sensations, remains preserved like a video recording in the center of this disc, unspoiled, as if frozen in ice. There, it is as if time does not exist. This heavy disc is the gravitational center of my memories. Today, every new experience is drawn there, forcing me into old reactions.

All our feelings and perceptions about the new events and world we face are shaped by these heavy discs. Images recorded in unresolved memories constantly rewind and replay around this dark center of gravity. They revolve around this center, like electrons revolving around an atom.

And each one of us experiences this world we live in through our unique perceptions and feelings, which are contained within the cores of these dark disks. In other words, our perception of the world is determined by the beliefs that form the core of the black holes in our minds. And these are formed and added there through the experiences we have in our lives. Until we complete our evolution, our soul tries to resolve these issues again and again in every life within the body it inhabits. Evolution, however, is achieved by accepting their existence as a crater and seeing them, rather than trying to fill the holes of these spiritual wounds and ignoring them. When we stop struggling with them, all that remains is to sit back, relax, and watch today pass by.

All these words and thoughts are now seeping into the infinite voids opening up in my mind. As if each one were a signal sent to a distant corner of the universe... Maybe one day, someone will pick them up and turn them into light in their own darkness. Or perhaps they will simply remain here, like an echo transcending time and space. But I know this: every step I take courageously within my inner world brings me one step closer to the silent call of a collective awakening. And perhaps this journey itself is the truest answer.

OBSERVER CONSCIOUSNESS

In first grade, my teacher slapped me hard for a little word joke I made. At that moment, she taught me in a painful and humiliating way that I must not cross the boundaries of society without permission from authority. And even years later, the echo of that slap still weighs heavily on me like a disc. Today, as I write these words, as I am about to appear before you, as I film these videos, I feel that inner restlessness... This is the echo of the past. Somewhere inside me, there is still the fear that my teacher will slap me for stepping into my friends' boundaries. The fear that my classmates will laugh at me follows immediately after. These lines are the echoes of my acceptance of confronting these fears.

Each new experience added a little more weight to that disc. My thoughts were like marbles spinning around that disc; each one carried the burden of the past onto today's shoulders. That slap from the teacher taught me that speaking in front of crowds was dangerous. Staying silent was safer. Expressing what was on my mind was punished by the authority.

Today I am 33 (35) years old. And I'm still trying to escape from the pull of that disc. Even my desire to participate in society is drawn to that center like a marble. I want to keep these things to myself, but that also means silencing the shadow within me.

In every bridge I build between the traces of the past and the present, I am both enchanted and anxious. I fear being misunderstood, but I know I am not alone; knowing that there are other people curious to explore their minds comforts me. When the masks that separate us are set aside, I believe this journey will be suitable for people, too. Because only then can it be understood that this is not my journey alone, but ours.

There is a part of me that believes in erasing the boundaries of my ego and achieving the peace of universal wholeness by becoming one with everyone. At the same time, I also have an identity that resists this, that tries to protect its current sense of self, its ego, by separating itself from everyone else. Protecting my ego takes me to a different place, while my desire for universal integration and unity requires me to go the opposite way. I suppose my life is spent in conflict between these two. Throughout the day, whichever side of me puts on the mask, I follow it. As I travel on journeys guided by the masks I wear during the day, I cannot tell whether I am following a chaotic, random path with no clear end or heading towards a specific point with a particular order. The Do-Loon-AI Express journey is a manifestation of my desire to grasp clues that could help me understand this within the labyrinth of the mind.

Every day the captains drive the car, while I, sitting here at midnight writing these words, watch the road from my own window. One of these captains drives for 9 of the 10 hours of the journey. My goal is to teach this captain to trust other captain so that he can lighten the load on it's shoulder. In other words, to learn to trust him. Both captains. As I make these edits right now, while the captains are asleep, I am thinking about this journey at the beginning of these lines.

Today, as I want to connect with "everyone" inside me, why have I been so afraid of being "like everyone else" for so many years? I hope to find the answer to this question in the following sections.

Maybe as a result of the preceding sections, I understand this better today. I feel myself beginning to shift into an observer consciousness. I was afraid of becoming like everyone else because, until now, I was stuck in the obsession of creating and maintaining my ego out of fear of its disappearance. Once again, I see that humans learn by making mistakes. Today, I understand that expending so much energy to prevent my ego from dissolving only weakened it. When I focus too much on the external image, I realize I cannot listen to my essence.

I aim to continue this journey through the dark shadows of my mind's forest, while holding a torch in my hand. For now, I am putting this long and winding inner journey into words. Perhaps these lines will be reborn when the time comes. Again and again. This way, whatever builds up inside me with every future contact here will possibly be alleviated a little.

Perhaps these lines are both therapy and an echo of the desire for liberation. I do not know where I will end up, at which boundary I will stop. But I know this: I will continue to listen to the voice of my soul. And this voice is calling me towards the most mysterious, most compelling shores of my existence.

Lunaris, 23 November 2022

-----

It has been 2.5 years since I recorded this first part. The pace and intensity of the therapy sessions had become very intense in recent times, and I would leave home at 9:00 and only manage to get back around 21:30. Lately, however, this intensity is gone, as if the universe is dancing with me, accompanying the changes in my mind and life. Where I need to slow down, it somehow slows me down. It gives me space. As I ponder the possible reasons for this, I recall those dark days when I couldn't remember what happened in the morning. Many of the clients I saw in the morning dropped out of the process. I don't remember what notes I took or what we talked about at those times. Only my morning clients know what I said and explained inside.

One of my clients told me that on some days, my ideas, thoughts, and attitude changed a lot. I wanted to get some details while I had the chance and asked them to remind me of the dialogue between us. With shallow thoughts far from depth, I had explained that everything in this life is actually meaningless, that we live like slaves, condemned to what society imposes on us. He said I emphasized that people are unreliable and that everything is pointless. I told him that he must accept that no one understands him and no one ever will, and that if he cannot accept this, his life has no meaning.

From these and similar clues, I realized that the person saying these things was a second identity within me, one I had denied for years, pushed into the shadows, and tried to control by recording everything I did to find it. I never imagined that one day I would experience the symptoms of "split identity" that I had listened to, talked about, observed, and studied in lectures for years.

Life feels very hazy during the day. This process has accelerated over the last 7-8 months. I decided to re-examine my life, as I wrote and narrated it in the original recordings of the Do-Loon-AI Express, to try to understand what I had missed. As I journeyed so deeply into my own inner self, I either disturbed it where it lay, prodding it to the surface, or I never came close to finding it, and my process ended in failure. I still don't know which is true. Perhaps this emotional pressure I am currently going through is the result of this process ending successfully. Therefore, I must take each step carefully and manage to achieve my inner balance and inner peace.

I want you to come here and give me answers. Let's make this place a living testament to our journey of becoming whole together. I have tried so hard to be you without you. You see what I said in my first recording; I have been searching for you from the very beginning. Doesn't that show you my good intentions? Please take a step towards me, too. When you do, write it with underlined letters and put your name and the time underneath; let there be no confusion. I can't wait to see what you have to say.

I'm waiting for you. This time, for real.

Lunaris, 5 February 2025, 20:47
chapters.where_en: The Black Disk zone (the Heavy Disk rite)
